chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Application No.D-36 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) Before: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Amjad Ali Sahito 16.08.2022. Mr. Meer Ahmed Mangrio, Advocate for applicants. Mr. Abdul Waheed Bijarani, Assistant P.G. O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J- Applicants were booked on 06.04.2022 and arrested in the present case on the basis of a Video Clip allegedly made viral on social media showing that applicants and others allegedly belonging to Jeay Sindh were making protest at Bypass Road, Thatta and raising slogans against Pakistan with flags of Sindu Desh. 2. During investigation, a USB containing such video clip was collected and sent to Forensic Science Laboratory (FSL) for a report. Learned Assistant Prosecutor General has informed that still the report has not been received and the witnesses whose 161 Cr.P.C statements in support have been recorded are not the eye witnesses but on the basis of hearsay information have implicated the applicants. We, therefore, are of the view that the case against applicants requires further inquiry as the only piece of evidence i.e. video clip with the prosecution has not been so far scientifically confirmed to be genuine or otherwise. No one who has allegedly seen the said video clip has been made witness in this case, in addition. Accordingly, this bail application is allowed and the applicants are admitted to post arrest bail, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand) each and P.R Bonds in the like amount to the satisfaction of learned trial Court. 3.

No one who has allegedly seen the said video clip has been made witness in this case, in addition. Accordingly, this bail application is allowed and the applicants are admitted to post arrest bail, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand) each and P.R Bonds in the like amount to the satisfaction of learned trial Court. 3. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not influence the trial Court while deciding the case on merits. JUDGE JUDGE Shahid
